(4) Remove the main pole mounting pins that secure the bimini top to the main pole mounting brackets. (5) Slowly pull the bimini top toward the bow and remove it from the boat.

question: "How do I install a bimini top on my boat if I want to use the canopy?"
ret: To install a bimini top for use with the canopy, follow these steps: (1) Ensure the bimini top is fully assembled and all components (e.g., main pole, forward support poles, lock pins, mounting brackets) are present. (2) Position the bimini top over the designated mounting points on your boat — align the main pole with the main pole mounting brackets and insert the main pole mounting pins securely. (3) Attach the forward support poles to their mounting brackets using the lock pins. (4) Raise the bimini top fully and verify all ratchets and connections are tight and stable. (5) Confirm the canopy is properly tensioned and centered under the frame. Note: Do not operate the boat at speeds exceeding 72 km/h (45 mph) with the bimini top raised. For detailed visuals or model-specific guidance (e.g., 210FSH DELUXE or 210FSH SPORT), refer to pages referenced in your owner’s manual.

question: "How to store the bimini top in the upright position of the boat?"
ret:
To store the bimini top in the upright position: (1) Remove the lock pins, and then push the center poles down. 

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTICES: 
• Do not exceed 72 km/h (45 mph) with the bimini top in the upright position. 
• Do not trailer the boat with the bimini top in the fully extended or upright storage position — always collapse it fully before trailering to avoid damage.

question: "When I am sailing, how do I check the engine oil level to ensure continued sailing?"
ret: When sailing, to safely and accurately check the engine oil level for continued operation: (1) Moor the boat securely so it remains stable and level on the water. (2) Stop the engine and wait a few minutes for oil to settle — but note: do NOT check immediately after shutdown if the engine is hot, as hot oil can cause burns. (3) Open the engine hood. (4) Locate the oil tank filler cap, loosen and remove it, then wipe the dipstick clean. (5) Reinsert the dipstick fully without screwing it in, then withdraw it to read the oil level against the minimum/maximum marks <PIC>. (6) If oil is below the minimum mark, add Yamalube-approved oil slowly; if above the maximum mark, consult a Yamaha Boat dealer. (7) Reinstall the filler cap securely and close the engine hood. ⚠️ Important: Never operate with too much or too little oil — this can severely damage the engine. , ["oil_level_dipstick_marks"]

question: "How can I use the bilge pump of the boat?"
ret: To use the bilge pump on your boat: 1. Locate the bilge pump switch (labeled '1 Bilge pump switch') and turn it ON — the pump will then operate manually. 2. Even if the switch is OFF, the bilge pump has an automatic function: it detects excessive water in the bilge and will activate to drain most of it through the bilge pump outlet <PIC>. 3. While operating, the bilge pump indicator light will illuminate <PIC>. 4. Note: The pump will not stop until most bilge water is drained. 5. IMPORTANT: Do not run engines at full throttle while bilge water remains in the engine compartment — water splashing into engines may cause severe damage. Also ensure all drain plugs are tightly secured before operation. For draining bilge water on land, remove the hull drain plug instead. Refer to page 81 for detailed bilge water draining instructions. , ["bilge001", "bilge002"]

question: "How to perform anchor light switch check while sailing?"
ret: To perform an anchor light switch check while sailing, follow these steps: (1) Ensure the anchor light is properly installed (for 210FSH DELUXE models, install it from the lockable storage compartment; see page 68). (2) Push the upper side of the navigation and anchor lights switch (labeled 'NAV') — both bow light and anchor light should illuminate. (3) Push the lower side of the same switch (labeled 'ANC') — only the anchor light should come on, confirming independent operation. (4) Return the switch to the middle (OFF) position — both lights must turn off. Also, verify that the 'HOUSE' switch remains ON (never OFF while moored), as turning it OFF disables the anchor light. If any light fails or circuit breaker trips, reset the breaker; if it trips again, consult a Boat Dealer for electrical system inspection.

question: "When I want to start the boat, how do I turn on the boat's engine to get the machine started?"
ret: To start the boat's engine safely and correctly: 1. Ensure the engine shut-off cord is attached to your left wrist and the clip is securely connected to the engine shut-off switch. 2. Return the remote control levers to the neutral position. 3. Turn the main switch key (for the relevant engine — port or starboard) to the ON position, then press and hold the START button for no more than 5 seconds. Do not press longer — continuous engagement can drain the battery or damage the starter. 4. If the engine does not start within 5 seconds, release the button, wait at least 15 seconds, then try again. 5. Once started, apply only enough throttle to maintain a fast idle — note that thrust is generated immediately upon engine start. <PIC> 6. Never turn the main switch key to START while the engine is already running — this may damage the starter mechanism. Also, avoid stopping the engine abruptly after high-speed operation; let it idle for a few minutes to cool first. , ["Manual40_10"]

question: "How do I turn a boat while sailing?"
ret: To turn a boat while sailing (specifically a jet-powered watercraft), follow these key principles: 1. You need throttle — steering only works when engine thrust is applied; the boat will not turn if engines are stopped or in neutral, even if the steering wheel is turned. 2. Turn the steering wheel in the direction you wish to go (e.g., right for starboard turn); this changes the angle of the jet thrust nozzles at the stern, redirecting thrust and pivoting the boat. 3. Remember that the stern swings outward opposite the turn direction — e.g., turning starboard causes the stern to swing left — so maintain safe clearance from people, docks, or obstacles. 4. Never touch the tilt lever during operation, and always ensure the steering wheel is locked after adjustment. 5. For precise low-speed maneuvering (e.g., docking), use idle or trolling speed with controlled throttle and short bursts of reverse if needed.

question: "How to operate the boat cross wakes and swells?"
ret: To safely cross wakes and swells: 1. Adjust your speed — slower speeds reduce impact. 2. Cross at an angle (ideally ~45°, known as "quartering") rather than head-on; this minimizes jolts and improves stability. 3. Be prepared for steering resistance — the boat may try to veer away from the wake/swell, especially at shallow angles (<10°). 4. Avoid jumping wakes or waves — it poses serious risk of spinal injury, facial trauma, or broken bones. 5. Small swells are easier to cross than large, sharp wakes; groups of wakes are more challenging than single ones. 6. Always maintain alertness, safe speed, and proper distance from other vessels and hazards.

question: "How do I load the boat when the cruise is over?"
ret: To load the boat after the cruise is over, follow these steps: (1) Disconnect the trailer lights from the towing vehicle. (2) Back the trailer down the ramp as close to 90° to the shoreline as possible—stop when the tops of the trailer’s fenders are about 3 inches above the waterline. Have a second person act as an observer if possible. (3) With the boat moving at the slowest idle speed, guide it straight onto the trailer’s support rails; use minimal throttle only if needed for steering control. (4) Ensure the boat is centered and aligned with the bow stop (bumper board); ease it forward gently until the bow rests fully against the bow stop. (5) Attach and tighten the winch line—but remember: the winch line alone is not sufficient for securing the bow during trailering; always use the chain in addition to the winch. After loading, perform post-operation checks including flushing the cooling system, draining residual water, washing the hull and jet drive, removing the access port cap and hull drain plug (then reinstalling the drain plug securely before next launch), rinsing the engine compartment (avoiding air filter and electrical components), applying rust inhibitor, covering the boat, and turning the battery switch to OFF.

question: "How to flush the cooling system of the boat?"
ret: To flush the cooling system of the boat, follow these steps: (1) Connect a garden hose to a water tap using the garden hose adapter. (2) Ensure the area around the boat is clear, then start the engine. Immediately after starting, fully turn on the water supply so water flows continuously from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet. ⚠️ Never turn on the water before starting the engine — this could cause water to backflow into the crankcase and severely damage the engine. (3) Run the engine at fast idle for 3–5 minutes. If the engine stops during flushing, turn off the water immediately and restart from step 2. (4) Turn off the water supply, then drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately pushing the remote control lever up to half-throttle and back to idle for 10–15 seconds. (5) Stop the engine. ⚠️ Never leave the water on while the engine is not running. Also, do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds after turning off the water to avoid overheating. (6) Remove the garden hose adapter and securely install/tighten the flush hose connector cap. Tip: If the cooling passages are dry, allow ~20 seconds after engine start for water to reach the pilot outlets; if no water appears, check for clogs in the intake grates (see 'Jet pump clean-out procedure').

question: "How to replace the fuse of the boat?"
ret: To replace the fuse of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Locate the fuse box on the right console next to the seat (Figure 70). 2. Identify the blown fuse — common fuses include: Electronic throttle valve fuse (10 A), Fuel pump fuse (10 A), Main relay drive fuse (10 A), Main fuse (20 A), Battery fuse (30 A), Accessory fuse (20 A), and Bilge pump fuse (3 A). 3. Use a fuse puller to carefully remove the blown fuse. 4. Replace it with a spare fuse of the **exact same amperage rating**. 5. Reinstall the fuse box cover; if replacing the accessory or bilge pump fuse, also reinstall the fuse holder. 6. Secure the fuse box to the air filter case and fasten the band. 7. Close the engine hood. ⚠️ Warning: Always check the circuit and connected component for malfunctions or shorts before replacing a fuse — repeated fuse blowing indicates an underlying issue.

question: "How to check the engine oil level of the boat?"
ret: To check the engine oil level of the boat, follow these steps: (1) With the engine stopped, place the boat in a precisely level position on land or launch it onto water. (2) Start the engine and run it at idling speed for at least 6 minutes (or 11 minutes if ambient temperature is 20°C/68°F or lower). (3) Stop the engine and wait for it to cool slightly — note: engine oil is extremely hot immediately after shutdown; avoid contact to prevent burns. (4) Open the engine hood. (5) Loosen and remove the oil tank filler cap, then wipe the attached dipstick clean. (6) Reinsert the dipstick fully without screwing it in, then withdraw it to check the oil level. The oil should be between the 'Minimum level mark' and 'Maximum level mark' on the dipstick. (7) If below minimum, slowly add recommended oil (e.g., Yamalube); if significantly above maximum, consult a boat dealer. (8) Repeat checking until the level is correct. (9) Securely reinstall the oil tank filler cap. (10) Repeat the procedure for the other engine (if equipped with dual engines). (11) Close the engine hood. Important: Do not run the engine with too much or too little oil — this may cause severe engine damage. Also ensure no debris or water enters the oil filler hole.

question: "How to use the energy saving mode of a coffee machine?"
ret: To use the energy saving mode of your coffee machine: 1. The machine automatically enters energy saving mode after 9 minutes of inactivity. 2. To wake it up and resume operation, press either the Espresso or Lungo button. 3. To manually exit energy saving mode (e.g., if you need immediate operation), press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds.

question: "How to change the default setting of the energy saving mode?"
ret:
要更改节能模式的默认设置，请按以下步骤操作：
1. 按下 Espresso 按钮进行设置：
　　• 按 1 次：启用节能关机（9 分钟无操作后自动进入待机）；
　　• 再按 1 次：改为 30 分钟后自动进入待机；
　　• 再按 1 次：关闭节能自动关机功能（即取消节能模式默认触发）。
该设置适用于配备节能功能的设备（如咖啡机类）。<PIC> , ["Manual01_21"]

question: "How to program the water valume of a coffee machine?"
ret: To program the water volume of your coffee machine: 1. Turn the machine on and wait for it to reach ready mode (steady lights). 2. Place a cup under the coffee outlet. 3. Press and hold the desired coffee button (e.g., Espresso or Lungo) until the desired volume is dispensed. 4. Release the button once the desired volume is served — the water volume level is now stored.

question: "How should I do if I want to empty the system before not in use, for frost protection or before maintenance?"
ret: To empty the system before a period of non-use, for frost protection, or before maintenance: 1. Press both the Espresso and Lungo buttons simultaneously to turn the machine off and enter emptying mode. 2. Then press the Lungo button and wait until the water tank is empty. NOTE: The machine will be blocked for 10 minutes after completing the emptying mode. <PIC> , ["Manual04_38"]

question: "How should I do if I want to reset to factory settings of the coffee machine?"
ret: To reset your coffee machine to factory settings: 1. Ensure the machine is turned off, then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds. 2. The LEDs will blink fast 3 times to confirm the reset has been completed. <PIC> , ["coffee0_01"]

question: "How should I clean my coffee maker after I use it so that it can last longer?"
ret: To clean your coffee maker properly and extend its lifespan, follow these key steps: 1. Always unplug the appliance before cleaning to avoid electric shock. 2. Never immerse the machine or any part of it in water. 3. Do not use strong cleaning agents, solvents, sharp objects, brushes, or abrasive materials. 4. Avoid dishwashers — never place the coffee maker or its components inside. 5. Clean the coffee outlet regularly with a soft, damp cloth. 6. Wipe the exterior surface using a mild detergent solution and a soft cloth. 7. For internal maintenance, descale the machine periodically using a recommended descaling agent (as per the descaling kit instructions) to prevent mineral buildup and ensure optimal performance. 8. Empty and rinse the water tank regularly — especially if unused for extended periods (e.g., holidays or weekends).

question: "How to open the swim platform of the boat\uff1f"
ret: To open the swim platform of the boat, follow these steps: (1) Locate the rear platform hatch — it is part of the swim platform assembly. (2) Pull the lock handle up. (3) Turn the lock handle clockwise to release the interlock mechanism. (4) Lift and open the rear platform hatch. Note: The swim platform may also serve as access to the wet storage compartment underneath. Ensure the hatch is fully latched when closing, as it has safety interlocks that shut off the engines if opened during operation.

question: "How to open the livewell and add water on the boat?"
ret: To open the livewell: Pull the latch to open the livewell lid. The livewell is located on the starboard side of the boat at the stern. To add water: Push the livewell switch to turn on the livewell pump and supply water. When there is sufficient water, push the switch again to turn off the pump. If needed, activate the aerator switch to circulate and oxygenate the water.

question: "What is the maintenance setting screen of the boat controller?"
ret: The maintenance setting screen of the boat controller displays the number of hours that the engines have been running since the last maintenance.

question: "In the boat's steering position, what does the factory reset screen show?"
ret: 您好，暂未查询到相关产品信息，已为您转接人工客服。

question: "What is the trip screen shown?"
ret: The trip screen displays information such as engine operating hours, fuel consumption, and other related metrics. It includes a menu button (1), a scrollbar (2), and a 'Reset' button (3). To reset a specific item, touch and hold it for several seconds; to reset all items, tap the 'Reset' button. Note that some items (e.g., total engine hours) cannot be reset. <PIC> , ["Manual29_30"]

question: "When I am ready to sail, if everything is ready, how do I make the boat move forward?"
ret: To make the boat move forward when everything is ready: 1. Ensure no boats, obstructions, or swimmers are nearby (WARNING! <PIC>). 2. Sit in a seat with feet on the deck and confirm all passengers are safely seated and holding on. 3. Start the engine. 4. Check all directions for safety. 5. Gently apply throttle (forward thrust) — remember: you need throttle to steer, and the boat will not respond to steering input without engine power (<PIC>). , ["warning_icon", "throttle_steering_note"]

question: "How do I check the boat's steering system when I'm driving it?"
ret: When driving the boat, you cannot perform a full pre-operation steering system check (which requires two people and is done on land), but you can monitor steering performance in real time: 1. Ensure the steering wheel/handlebars are securely locked in position and show no looseness or free play during operation. 2. Turn the wheel/handlebars fully left and right while underway at low-to-moderate speed to confirm smooth, unrestricted response — the boat should turn promptly without binding or delay. 3. Remember that steering depends on jet thrust: throttle must be above idle (except at trolling speed) for effective directional control. 4. Watch for abnormal behavior — such as delayed response, uneven turning, or unintended drift — which may indicate steering system issues. 5. Never touch the tilt lever while operating, as it could cause sudden steering changes and accidents. If any irregularity is observed, stop safely and inspect or contact service support.

question: "How should fire extinguishers be stored on board and where should they be placed?"
ret: Fire extinguishers on board should be stored securely and in designated, accessible locations as follows: 1. One fire extinguisher (chemical-type, ≥2 lb capacity, B-1/5-B classification) must be mounted in the lockable storage compartment. 2. If carrying two extinguishers, the second should be mounted in the battery compartment — this one should be a 'clean agent' type (e.g., CO₂ or FE-36™), ≥5 lb capacity, to safely suppress engine compartment fires. 3. Always keep extinguishers inside their designated containers (e.g., fire extinguisher container $\textcircled{1}$ in the battery compartment <PIC>), with caps tightened securely. 4. Never store extinguishers loosely; they must remain readily accessible and upright. 5. For engine fires, use the FIRE-PORT™ below the leaning post to discharge the clean agent extinguisher into the engine compartment without opening the hatch — this prevents oxygen influx and explosion risk. Always follow the manufacturer’s instructions for inspection and maintenance. , ["EJU01037", "EJU01838"]

question: "How to care the throttle-cable of the boat?"
ret: To care for the throttle cable of your boat, follow these key guidelines: 1. **Do NOT grease the throttle cable itself** — greasing the cable housing or inner cable can cause it to freeze, leading to loss of throttle control. As stated in the manual: 'Apply a dab of grease to the cable end only. Do not grease the brake/throttle cables themselves because they could become frozen, which could cause loss of control.' 2. **Inspect regularly** for kinks, fraying, corrosion, or binding — especially at attachment points and where the cable passes through guides or grommets. 3. **Ensure proper adjustment**: Before adjusting the throttle cable, confirm engine idle speed is correctly set. Then loosen the adjuster lock nut and turn the adjuster until the throttle lever has the specified free play (refer to your model’s service manual for exact specification). 4. **Keep clean and dry**: After use — especially in saltwater — rinse the engine compartment with fresh water (avoiding electrical components and air filter), and inspect the throttle linkage area for debris or salt buildup. 5. **Lubricate only as directed**: If lubrication is needed at pivot points (e.g., throttle lever shaft or remote control bracket), use marine-grade lubricant sparingly — never on the cable core or housing.

question: "How do I charge the camera battery when I'm not using it?"
ret: 当不使用相机时，建议将电池从相机中取出，并为其安装保护盖以防短路。充电时，请仅使用兼容的电池组（如BP-511A、BP-514、BP-511或BP-512），并将电池放入专用充电器中进行充电；也可通过USB线将相机直接连接至电脑充电（请勿通过键盘或USB集线器连接）<PIC>。注意：若电脑进入睡眠模式，充电会暂停，需唤醒电脑并重新连接USB线以继续充电。出厂时电池未充满，请首次使用前务必充电。充电完成后红灯亮起，建议继续充电约1小时以确保完全充满。 , ["Manual29_17"]

question: "How to install the camera battery when another one is power off?"
ret: When the camera is powered off, follow these steps to install the battery: 1. Open the battery compartment cover. 2. Align the battery contacts downward and match the battery’s front edge with the <<>> mark (if present on the compartment). 3. Insert the battery firmly until it locks into place <PIC>. 4. Ensure the battery compartment cover is fully closed — failure to do so may prevent power-on. Note: Only use compatible batteries (e.g., BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512); using incorrect batteries may cause power issues. , ["Manual01_3"]

question: "How do I connect the camera using a household power socket?"
ret:
To connect the camera using a household power socket, you’ll need the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2 (includes AC adapter, DC coupler, and power cord). Steps are as follows:
1. Insert the DC Coupler: Open the battery compartment cover and the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler fully until it locks in place, then route the cord through the notch and close both covers.
2. Connect the DC Coupler to the AC adapter: Plug the DC Coupler’s plug into the AC adapter’s socket.
3. Plug the AC adapter into a household power outlet (AC 100–240 V compatible).
⚠️ Important: Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera’s power switch is turned ON.
Note: This setup eliminates battery-level concerns during extended use. <PIC> , ["Manual29_17"]

question: "How do you mount the lens of a camera when preparing for photography?"
ret:
安装相机镜头的步骤如下：
1. 确保相机电源已关闭；
2. 移除镜头后盖和相机机身卡口盖；
3. 将EF-S镜头对准相机机身上的白色EF-S镜头安装标记（<PIC>），沿箭头方向旋转镜头直至听到“咔嗒”声，表示已牢固锁定；
4. 若使用非EF-S镜头（如EF镜头），请对准机身上的红色EF镜头安装标记进行安装。
提示：安装过程中请一手稳固持握相机机身，另一手托住镜头底部，避免镜头重量对卡口造成损伤。 , ["Manual29_36"]

question: "How do you install the card into a camera before photography?"
ret: To install the CF card into the camera before photography, follow these steps: 1. Open the CF card cover by sliding it as indicated by the arrow in the manual. 2. Ensure the CF card is oriented correctly: face the label side toward you, and insert the end with the small holes first — inserting it incorrectly may damage the camera. 3. Gently push the card in until it clicks into place; the CF card eject button will pop out when fully inserted. 4. Before first use (or if the card was formatted elsewhere), format it in the camera via the menu ([Format] under Setup) — note that formatting erases all data. <PIC> , ["cf_card_insertion"]

question: "Have you ever wondered how to remove the camera shutter button? Understanding this process can enhance your photography experience and allow for quick repairs!"
ret: 您好，相机的‘快门按钮’（shutter button）是机身外部的不可拆卸操作部件，并非可移除或需用户自行拆解的模块。它采用一体化设计，用于控制对焦（半按）和曝光拍摄（全按），不具备用户可拆卸结构。强行尝试拆除将导致设备损坏、失去保修资格，甚至引发内部电路或机械结构损伤。如您遇到快门按钮失灵、卡滞或无响应等问题，建议立即停止使用，并联系官方售后服务中心进行专业检测与维修。

question: "How do I replace the camera's date/time battery?"
ret: To replace the camera's date/time battery (a CR2016 lithium battery), follow these steps: 1. Turn the power switch to <OFF>. 2. Open the battery compartment cover — slide the lever as indicated by the arrow and lift the cover. 3. Remove the battery holder. 4. Replace the old CR2016 battery in the holder, ensuring correct +/− orientation. 5. Reinsert the battery holder and close the cover securely. Note: The date/time setting will be reset after replacement, so you must manually reconfigure the correct date and time via the menu (< > button). The backup battery has a service life of approximately 5 years.

question: "How to set an off-center subject mode of a camera?"
ret: To set an off-center subject mode, use the camera's autofocus (AF) system to select a specific AF point over your subject instead of relying on the center point only. Look through the viewfinder, navigate and activate an off-center AF point (using the multi-controller or cross keys), position it over your subject, then press the shutter button halfway to lock focus. Once focused, you can recompose the frame while keeping focus locked (known as 'focus-and-recompose'). Note: Ensure AF mode is set to <ONE SHOT> for still subjects. For best results with off-center composition, avoid using Basic Zone modes like Portrait or Night Portrait, as they auto-select AF points and settings; instead, use Creative Zone modes (e.g., P, Av, Tv, or M) for full control. <PIC> , ["Manual29_18"]

question: "Before taking photos, we need to know How to use the eyepiece cover of the camera?"
ret: To use the eyepiece cover correctly: 1. Remove the eyecup first — push it upward from the bottom. 2. Slide the eyepiece cover down into the eyepiece groove to attach it securely. The eyepiece cover is especially important during self-timer or remote shooting to prevent stray light from entering the viewfinder and affecting exposure. Note: The eyepiece cover is also attached to the camera strap (p.89). <PIC> , ["Manual29_36"]

question: "How do I select the AF Mode when before taking a picture?"
ret: To select the AF Mode before taking a picture, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the lens focus mode switch is set to <AF> (as stated in Reference Doc 5). 2. Set the camera Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode (P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP) — in Basic Zone modes, AF mode is selected automatically and cannot be manually changed. 3. Press the <MENU> button, navigate to the AF menu (or use Quick Control screen), and choose one of the three AF modes: • One-Shot AF — for still subjects; • AI Servo AF — for continuously moving subjects; • AI Focus AF — automatically switches between One-Shot and AI Servo if subject movement is detected (Reference Docs 4 & 5). Note: AF mode selection is only available in Creative Zone modes; Basic Zone modes (e.g., Full Auto, Portrait, Sports) set AF mode automatically based on scene detection.

question: "How to set the camera model to \"P\" model?"
ret:
To set the camera model to "P" (Program Auto) mode, locate the Mode Dial on your camera and rotate it to the position marked

.

question: "After or during shooting, how can I view the camera image on TV for better clarity?"
ret: To view captured images on a TV for better clarity after or during shooting: 1. Ensure both the camera and TV are turned OFF before connecting. 2. Open the camera's terminal cover and use the provided video cable to connect the camera's VIDEO OUT terminal to the TV's VIDEO IN terminal—insert the plug fully. 3. Turn on the TV and switch its input source to 'Video IN'. 4. Set the camera's TV output switch to <ON> (or as indicated in your model), then press the playback (▶) button—the image will appear on the TV screen (the camera's LCD will not display anything). 5. You can browse single images, index display, magnified view, or shooting info using the camera's controls. Always turn off the camera and TV before disconnecting the cable.

question: "How can I delete a single image from my camera if I don't like the photo?"
ret:
要删除相机中单张不喜欢的照片，请按以下步骤操作：
1. 浏览照片，显示您想要删除的那张图像；
2. 按下确认/功能按钮（如参考文档6所述），屏幕底部将弹出“Erase”擦除菜单；
3. 使用拨盘选择[Erase]选项，然后按下确认键；
4. 确认后，访问指示灯会闪烁，图像即被删除 <PIC>。
⚠️注意：已设为“受保护”的图像无法直接删除，需先取消保护；删除后不可恢复，请谨慎操作。 , ["Manual29_62"]

question: "How can I erase all images from my camera if I don't like these."
ret: To erase all images from your camera: 1. Turn the dial to select [OK] or [All] in the Erase menu, then press the button; all unprotected images will be erased. Note: Protected images will remain unless you first cancel protection. For complete erasure (including protected images), format the CF card instead—select [Format] in the menu, confirm, and proceed (this will delete everything permanently). Remember: Erased images cannot be recovered, so ensure you've backed up any important ones first.

question: "How do I print photos using the camera's CP direct method after taking them?"
ret:
使用相机的CP Direct方法打印照片，请按以下步骤操作：
1. 拍摄照片后，确保相机已通过专用接口线连接至支持CP Direct的打印机；
2. 在相机菜单中，进入[Communication]设置项，将[ft Communication]设为[PTP] <PIC>；
3. 返回主菜单，选择[Print Order]，然后按SET键进入打印设置界面；
4. 使用主拨盘选择要打印的照片（支持单张或批量选择），确认后进入打印设置屏；
5. 设置[Style]、[Print Type]（如[Index]可一次打印20张信用卡尺寸图像）等选项；
6. 旋转主拨盘选择[OK]，按SET键开始打印；
7. 打印过程中如需停止，当屏幕显示[Stop]时按SET键，再选[OK]确认。注意：若中途断电、更换纸盒或删除待印图像，则无法恢复打印。
提示：打印前请确保电池电量充足，并仅使用原装接口线连接设备。 , ["Manual29_31"]

question: "How do you set up the camera to fine tune the model when using it?"
ret: 您好，关于‘如何设置相机以微调模型’的问题，当前提供的资料中未明确涉及‘模型微调（model fine-tuning）’这一AI/机器学习场景下的相机使用方式。您所描述的操作通常适用于AI视觉训练中的数据采集环节，但该功能并非消费级相机的标准设置流程。参考文档中提及的设置包括：AF/测光/驱动模式选择、图像质量（JPEG/RAW）、对焦锁定、三脚架辅助、构图与持机稳定性等基础拍摄准备步骤 <PIC>。若您是指为训练AI模型采集高质量标注图像，建议：1. 使用‘手动（M）’或‘光圈优先（Av）’模式确保曝光一致；2. 开启网格线辅助构图；3. 固定机位并使用三脚架减少抖动；4. 设置高分辨率JPEG+RAW双格式保存原始数据。如需具体机型的SDK开发或API接入支持，请提供相机型号以便进一步协助。 , ["Manual29_36"]

question: "After the earphones is in my hand, what are the components I should have?"
ret:
您好，当您拿到耳机电产品时，标准包装内应包含以下组件：
1. 左右真无线耳塞（Earbuds）×2
2. 充电盒（Charging Case）×1（内置600mAh电池，可为耳塞额外充电约3次）
3. USB充电线 ×1（用于为充电盒供电）
4. 多尺寸硅胶耳塞套（通常含S/M/L三对，用于适配不同耳道）
5. 用户手册 ×1（含安全须知、操作指南及保修信息）

⚠️温馨提示：请在首次使用前，先用USB线为充电盒充满电（约1小时），再将耳塞放入盒中激活配对。如发现缺少任一配件，请及时联系客服为您补发。

如需查看各部件图示，可参考说明书中的‘Front view’‘Bottom view’及‘Accessories’章节对应图片 <PIC>。 , ["earphones_01", "earphones_02", "earphones_03"]

question: "If the case battery of earphones is low, how can I charge is?"
ret: To charge the earphone charging case battery: 1. Use the provided USB-C cable and insert it into the charging port located at the back of the charging case. 2. Connect the other end to a USB power adapter plugged into an electrical outlet. When charging, the case's battery indicator will light up; if it blinks white 3 times with 3 ascending tones, charging is active. <PIC> , ["drill0_09"]

question: "When bluetooth are pairing and connecting, what scenarios I might encounter?"
ret: During Bluetooth pairing and connecting, you may encounter the following common scenarios: 1. **Automatic reconnection**: If the earbuds were previously paired with a nearby device, they will auto-reconnect to it instead of entering pairing mode — to avoid this, turn Bluetooth OFF on that device first. 2. **Entering pairing mode**: When you open the charging case, the earbuds connect to each other first, then enter pairing mode (indicated by <PIC> both LEDs blinking). 3. **Manual pairing trigger**: Press and hold either left or right earbud to drop current connection and force pairing mode. 4. **Multipoint setup**: To pair with a second device (e.g., phone + laptop), turn Bluetooth OFF on the first connected device — earbuds will disconnect and become discoverable; after pairing the second device, turning Bluetooth back ON on the first device enables auto-switching between them. 5. **LED behavior cues**: Both LEDs blinking simultaneously indicates pairing mode <PIC>; both LEDs blinking alternately <PIC> may indicate a different status (e.g., reset or synchronization — refer to full manual for exact meaning). 6. **Failed/intermittent pairing**: Ensure Bluetooth is enabled on your phone/device, and the earbuds are not already connected to another active device. , ["earbud0_01", "earbud0_02", "earbud0_03"]

question: "What are the two main controling functions of earphones?"
ret: The two main controlling functions of the earphones are: 1. Music control (e.g., Play, Pause, Next, Volume Up/Down) via physical buttons or voice commands; 2. Mode control (e.g., toggling Ambient Awareness ON/OFF and Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) ON/OFF) via earbud button presses.

question: "Besides the two main controling functions, what other functions of the earphones can I operate?"
ret: Besides the two main controlling functions, your earphones support several additional features: 1. Voice control — after enabling in the app, say 'Hey assistant' followed by commands like 'Play', 'Pause', 'Next', 'Volume Up/Down', 'Ambient ON/OFF', 'Noise Canceling ON/OFF', 'Spotify', 'Assistant', or 'End Call'; incoming calls can be accepted/rejected with just 'Accept' or 'Reject'. 2. Multipoint Bluetooth connection — allows simultaneous pairing with two devices (e.g., smartphone and laptop) for seamless switching between music and calls. 3. Ambient awareness & ANC mode cycling — press the left earbud to toggle between ambient sound mode and noise canceling mode. 4. Hardware reset — press and hold for 10 seconds if earbuds become unresponsive. 5. App-based customization — via the official app, you can customize button actions, enable hands-free voice control, browse advanced features, and update firmware for optimal performance.

question: "If my earphones won't work, how can I reset them?"
ret: If your earphones won't work, you can try a hardware reset first: press and hold the earbuds for 10 seconds to restart them. This is recommended only if they’re unresponsive or locked up (e.g., flashing red and blue lights). For a factory reset — which erases all settings — ensure the earbuds are disconnected from Bluetooth and removed from the charging case, then press and hold either earbud for 6 seconds. After resetting, you may need to re-pair them with your device via Bluetooth.

question: "What are the maintenance and care policies of the earphones?"
ret:
关于耳机的维护与保养政策，具体如下：
1. 清洁方法：请使用柔软干布或纸巾擦拭耳塞及充电盒，切勿使用任何强效化学清洁剂、溶剂或湿布；清洁前请确保耳塞已完全干燥。
2. 存放要求：不使用时，请将耳机存放于阴凉、干燥处，避免阳光直射；切勿暴露于高温（＞100°F / 37.8°C）或极寒环境中。
3. 使用防护：请勿将耳机浸水、靠近水源或置于潮湿表面；避免跌落、挤压或施加过度物理外力；切勿自行拆解或维修，如有故障请联络授权服务中心。
4. 充电安全：充电时请保持充电线通风，远离易燃材料（如床单、化纤织物）；切勿拉扯、猛拽连接线缆，应轻柔插拔。
5. 环保处置：本产品含可回收材料，请依据当地法规进行分类回收；建议联系本地回收机构或制造商获取进一步处置指导。
6. 防护等级：具备IP55级防汗防尘能力，但不适用于浸泡或高压水洗。

question: "From three different observing views, what buttons and interfaces should this eReader have?"
"What do the functions of Main Menu and Browser History of the eReader can do?"
ret: The 'Main Menu' serves as the central navigation hub of the eReader, providing access to core functions such as eBook reading, browser mode, bookmarks management (Save/Load/Del Mark), Page Jump, Flip Time, Brightness, and Set Color settings. It is accessed via the 'M' (Menu) button during eBook mode or from the home interface. The 'Browser History' displays a list of recently browsed files (e.g., EPUB, PDF, TXT), allowing you to quickly resume reading from the last page viewed in each book — simply navigate to an entry and press the 'M' button to open it at that exact position. Note: if a file was stored on a Micro SD card and later removed, its record remains in Browser History but cannot be opened.

question: "When in eBook mode of this eReader, what will happen after pressing \"M\" botton?"
ret: When in eBook mode, pressing the "M" button opens a menu with multiple functions: "Page Jump", "Save Mark", "Load Mark", "Del Mark", "Browser Mode", "Flip Time", "Brightness", and "Set Color".

question: "If I want to listen to music using this eReader, what should I do?"
ret: To listen to music on your eReader, follow these steps: 1. Transfer supported audio files (MP3, WMA, WAV, ACC, ECT) to the device by connecting it to your computer via USB cable — it will appear as a removable disk; drag and drop files into the internal memory or Micro SD card (if inserted). 2. From the main menu, open the "Music" section (refer to page 8 of the manual). 3. Select your desired audio file and press "M" to enter music play mode. 4. Use the "Play/Pause" button to start or pause playback; adjust volume with the Up/Down keys; skip tracks using the left/right navigation keys. You can also listen to music while reading an eBook or browsing photos. For private listening, plug in headphones via the 3.5mm earphone jack <PIC>. Note: Built-in speaker is also available. , ["eReader_06"]

question: "Can this eReader record voice? If so, how do I operate this feature?"
ret: Yes, this eReader supports voice recording. To use this feature: 1. Go to the Main Menu; 2. Select "Record"; 3. Press the "M" button to enter voice record mode. For more details, refer to page 13 of the user manual titled "Voice Recording".

question: "I can't find the way to play video using this eReader, can you tell me how to do that?"
ret: To play video on your eReader, please follow these steps: 1. Go to the "Video" section from the main menu. 2. Press the "M" key to enter Video mode. 3. Select the desired video file from the list and press "M" again to start playback. Supported video formats include AVI, RMVB, MPEG2, and MP4. Before playing, ensure your video files are properly transferred to the device — connect it to your computer via USB cable, then copy the videos into the internal memory or Micro SD card (if inserted).

question: "Can this eReader become a photo viewer? If so, how do I operate this feature?"
ret: Yes, this eReader can function as a photo viewer. It supports JPG, GIF, and PNG image formats. To view photos: 1. Ensure your images are copied to the device’s internal memory or inserted Micro SD card (up to 16GB). 2. From the Main Menu, select "Photo" <PIC>. 3. Use the navigation keys (↑ ↓ ← →) to browse thumbnails, and press the "M" key to open and view a selected image. 4. While viewing, you can zoom (if supported), scroll around magnified images, or use the "Home/ESC" button to return. For best results, store images in a dedicated folder on the device. , ["Manual13_0"]

question: "When using this eReader, what kinds of trobles might I encounter and how to solve them?"
ret:
When using this eReader, you may encounter the following common issues and their solutions:

1. **Cannot power on**: 
   - Charge the device fully via USB cable until the power indicator bar is full. 
   - If still unresponsive, the device may be defective—contact manufacturer for support.

2. **Cannot charge**: 
   - Check if the AC adapter or USB cable is defective; contact manufacturer for replacement.

3. **No sound or noise with earphones**: 
   - Adjust volume (ensure it’s not set to 0). 
   - Try a different pair of earphones. 
   - Use high-quality audio files—low-bitrate files may cause distortion.

4. **Cannot download or transfer files to the eReader**: 
   - Verify the USB cable is functional. 
   - Ensure internal memory or Micro SD card (if inserted) is not full. 
   - For Windows 98 users: Install a USB 2.0 driver to recognize the device as a removable disk.

5. **Micro SD card not recognized**: 
   - Power off the device, remove and reinsert the card carefully. 
   - ⚠️ Important: Always power off before inserting/removing the card; never reset the unit with card inserted.

6. **Screen display issues (e.g., fading, slow response)**: 
   - Avoid use outside the operating temperature range (32–120°F / 0–50°C), especially in cold outdoor conditions.
   - Keep away from moisture, dust, direct sunlight, and extreme heat.

7. **Music/video playback failure**: 
   - This eReader supports most common formats (MP3, WAV, AVI, RMVB, etc.), but some newer or high-resolution formats may not be compatible. Contact tech support for format-specific guidance.

For firmware updates, visit the manufacturer’s website. Always refer to the official user manual and contact authorized support—not self-repair—if issues persist.

question: "When connecting the fax, hwo can I properly finish the procedure?"
ret: To properly finish connecting your fax machine, follow these key steps: 1. Ensure the fax is placed near a telephone wall jack and a grounded AC power outlet. 2. Use only the provided standard modular telephone cord (RJ11C) to connect the fax to the telephone line — do not use non-compliant or damaged cords. 3. Plug the power cord fully into a grounded outlet; never use extension cords or unapproved power adapters. 4. Verify the Ringer Equivalence Number (REN) of your fax and all other connected devices does not exceed 5.0 on the same line — excessive REN may prevent ringing. 5. After physical connections, power on the device and configure essential settings (e.g., date/time and Station ID) via the control panel or Online User's Guide. For detailed step-by-step instructions, refer to the 'Quick Setup Guide' included with your model. If the fax fails to register a dial tone or send/receive test faxes, check all cable connections, ensure no other devices are interfering, and confirm your telephone line supports fax service.

question: "What should I pay attention to in order to ensure my safety when using this fax?"
ret: To ensure your safety when using this fax machine, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. **Electrical Safety**: Use only the provided power cord and plug it into a grounded AC outlet; never use damaged, frayed, or unauthorized cords or extension cords. Unplug the device immediately if it becomes unusually hot, emits smoke or strong odors, or if liquid is spilled on it. 2. **Placement & Environment**: Place the fax on a flat, stable surface in a dry, well-ventilated area—away from water sources (e.g., sinks, bathtubs), direct sunlight, heat sources, flammable materials, and corrosive chemicals. Maintain operating conditions of 50–95°F (10–35°C) and 20–80% humidity with no condensation. 3. **Physical Safety**: Do not place objects on or near the fax—especially metal items, containers with liquid, or heavy objects—as they may cause short circuits, fire, or electrical shock. Keep plastic packaging away from children to avoid suffocation risk. 4. **Operation Precautions**: Avoid using the fax during thunderstorms; do not use it to report gas leaks; never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the line is unplugged. Do not insert foreign objects into slots or openings. 5. **Servicing & Maintenance**: Never open or attempt to service the unit yourself—high-voltage components inside pose serious shock risks and may void warranty. Refer all servicing to an authorized Brother Service Center. 6. **Emergency Access**: Ensure the power cord remains easily accessible so you can unplug it quickly in case of emergency.

question: "In order to keep your fingers safe, what should you pay attention to about the fax?"
ret: To keep your fingers safe when using the fax, be careful not to put your fingers in the areas shown in the illustrations — these are typically paper feed paths, output trays, or internal mechanisms where moving parts or pinch points exist. Wait until pages have fully exited the product before picking them up, as reaching in prematurely may trap your fingers. Also, never insert your hand or any foreign objects into slots or openings (e.g., ink insertion slot, paper path), as this may cause injury. <PIC> , ["fax_01"]

question: "I'm about to move this fax. Is there anything I should pay attention to?"
ret: When moving your fax machine, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. **Power & Cables**: Unplug the AC power cord and telephone line (RJ-11) before moving — never pull on cords; grasp plugs directly. Ensure no cables are pinched, stretched, or damaged during transport. 2. **Trays & Paper**: Remove all paper trays (especially if extended for large paper) to prevent tipping or injury — the weight of paper can cause trays to collapse or the unit to become unstable <PIC>. 3. **Physical Handling**: Carry the unit upright on a flat, stable surface; avoid tilting or laying it on its side after ink cartridges are installed (to prevent leakage). Keep hands away from moving parts (e.g., paper path, scanner mechanism) — these are marked in gray-shaded areas <PIC>. 4. **Environment**: Place the fax in a dry, level location with temperature between 50–95°F (10–35°C), humidity 20–80%, away from water, direct sunlight, heat sources, magnets, or corrosive chemicals. 5. **Safety Precautions**: Do not place objects on top or around the unit — ensure easy access to the power outlet for emergency shutdown. Avoid using extension cords; use only the provided grounded power cord. For full safety details, refer to the Quick Setup Guide and Online User's Guide. , ["fax_04", "fax_05"]

question: "Are caution or warning labels inside the fax important? If not, can I remove them?"
ret: Yes, caution or warning labels inside the fax are important. They provide critical safety information to prevent injury, electrical shock, fire, or equipment damage. According to the safety documentation: 'DO NOT remove or damage the caution or warning label inside the product.' Removing them may void your warranty, compromise safety compliance, and increase risk of harm or malfunction.

ret: 您好，关于您的问题：加拿大地区支持该传真机的正常使用。产品已通过Industry Canada（加拿大创新、科学与经济发展部）的合规认证（RSS-210、ICES-003 Class B），符合加拿大无线电干扰及电磁兼容法规，适用于加拿大市场。所有相关说明书、保修政策及技术支持服务均在加拿大有效。您可通过brand of the fax加拿大官网或授权服务中心获取本地化用户手册、驱动程序及客户服务支持。如需纸质版《在线用户指南》，可联系品牌客服申请邮寄。

question: "When using the grill, how to connect regulator to the LP Tank?"
ret:
To connect the regulator to the LP tank when using the grill, follow these steps:
1. Ensure the LP tank is properly secured onto the grill (refer to the assembly section).
2. Turn all control knobs to the OFF position.
3. Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop.
4. Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve — always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve.
5. Hold the regulator and insert its nipple squarely into the LP tank valve, ensuring the nipple is centered <PIC>.
6. Hand-tighten the coupling nut while holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve (to avoid cross-threading). Then turn the coupling nut clockwise to a full stop — the regulator seals via the back-check feature in the valve, so expect resistance; complete the connection with an additional half to three-quarters turn by hand only (do not use tools). If connection fails, disconnect and repeat steps 5–6; if still unsuccessful, do not use this regulator.
⚠️ Safety note: Before opening the LP tank valve, verify the coupling nut is tight. Always perform a leak check after connection (see Use & Care section). , ["grill0_05"]

question: "To engure my safety when using the grill, how to do leak testing on valves, hose and regulatol?"
ret: To ensure your safety, perform a leak test on valves, hose, and regulator before each use or after any disconnection/reconnection. Follow these steps: 1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF. 2. Ensure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank. 3. Slowly open the LP tank valve (turn OPD hand wheel counterclockwise). If you hear a rushing sound, close the valve immediately — this indicates a major leak at the connection; contact Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME before proceeding. 4. Apply a soapy water solution (e.g., dish soap + water) to all connections: valve outlet, hose ends, regulator inlet/outlet, and hose surface. Watch for bubbles — any bubbling indicates a leak. <PIC> 5. If a leak is detected, STOP using the grill immediately. Close the LP tank valve fully (clockwise), ventilate the area, and do not operate any ignition sources. Call your LP gas supplier or fire department if the leak cannot be stopped. Never insert tools or foreign objects into valves — this may damage seals and cause leaks. Also, inspect hoses before each use for cuts, wear, or kinks; replace if damaged. Use only the valve/hose/regulator specified in the Parts List. , ["leak_test_diagram"]

question: "What should I pay attention to on ablout indirect cooking using the grill?"
ret: When using indirect cooking on your grill, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. Always keep the grill lid closed during indirect cooking to maintain consistent heat circulation and ensure even, slow roasting. 2. Use only selected burners (not all) to generate gentle, circulating heat — avoid direct flame contact with food, especially for poultry or large, fatty cuts, to minimize flare-ups. 3. Adjust temperature settings upward in cold or windy conditions to compensate for heat loss and maintain sufficient cooking temperatures. 4. Ensure proper ventilation — use the grill only in open, well-ventilated outdoor areas, maintaining at least 3 ft. from walls and 10 ft. from flammable objects or ignition sources. 5. Never use charcoal or ceramic briquets in a gas grill unless specifically supplied and approved for your model.

question: "What are the first three steps in assembly process of the grill according to the manual?"
ret: 您好，根据提供的手册内容，未明确列出组装流程的完整步骤编号（如第1、2、3步），也未找到清晰标注的‘前三个组装步骤’的说明。手册中提及的部分组装相关操作包括：1. 准备工具（可调扳手、螺丝刀、7/16"组合扳手）；2. 将烤架向前倾斜后抬起取出（可能涉及底座或部件定位）；3. 将烤架头部安装到推车架上（需两人协作，对齐槽口与立柱，并确保调节阀软管置于推车内侧）。但这些描述分散在不同章节，且无明确‘Step 1/2/3’序号标识。建议您查阅手册中‘Assembly’标题下的图示步骤页，或拨打手册所列服务热线1-800-241-7548获取分步指导。

question: "Can you share me some safety tips using the grill?"
ret:
Certainly! Here are key safety tips for using your grill safely:

1. **Location & Ventilation**: Use the grill only in a well-ventilated, open outdoor area — NEVER in enclosed spaces like garages, porches, carports, or under overhead structures.

2. **Clearance & Setup**: Place the grill at least 3 ft. from any wall or surface, and maintain a 10-ft. clearance from flammable objects (e.g., deck railings, overhanging branches, pilot lights, or electrical appliances).

3. **Children & Pets**: Keep children and pets away from the grill during operation and while it’s still hot.

4. **Gas Safety**: 
   - Always check coupling nut tightness before opening the LP tank valve.
   - Turn off all control knobs AND the LP tank valve when not in use.
   - Never move the grill while operating or still hot.
   - Perform a leak test before first use and after any disconnection.
   - Do NOT attach to RV/motorhome LP systems.

5. **Fire Prevention**: 
   - Keep the grill area clear of combustible materials.
   - Never use water on a grease fire — instead, turn off all knobs and the LP tank immediately.
   - Empty the grease tray after each use (only when fully cooled) to prevent flare-ups.
   - Clean burners and grates regularly to avoid grease buildup.

6. **Personal Protection**: Use long-handled utensils and heat-resistant oven mitts to avoid burns and splatters.

7. **Food Safety**: 
   - Wash hands, utensils, and surfaces before/after handling raw meat.
   - Use a food thermometer: poultry (165°F), ground meats (160°F), steaks/roasts (145°F), pork (160°F).
   - Never partially grill and finish later — cook thoroughly in one session.

8. **Storage**: Store outdoors covered and dry; if storing indoors, disconnect and remove the LP tank and store it outdoors separately.

For further assistance, contact the Grill Service Center at 1-800-241-7548.

question: "What are identification numbers of my jetski? I don't understand that."
ret: Your jetski has three main identification numbers: 1. Primary Identification (PRI-ID) number — stamped on a label inside the engine compartment <PIC>; 2. Hull Identification Number (HIN) — a unique 12-character ID assigned to the hull; 3. Engine serial number — located on the engine itself. These numbers are essential for ordering parts, registering your watercraft, or reporting theft. Please refer to your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual for exact locations and recording spaces. , ["jetski_01"]

question: "I've heard that my jetski has cruising limitations. Please introduce them."
ret: Your jetski has several important cruising limitations designed for safety and optimal performance: 1. **Occupancy & Load Limit**: This watercraft is designed for the operator and **one passenger only**—never exceed **2 persons** or the **maximum load limit of 160 kg (353 lb)**, which includes the combined weight of operator, passenger, and all cargo. 2. **Operator Experience**: Do not operate with a passenger until you have considerable solo riding experience—passenger operation requires greater skill and familiarity with handling characteristics. 3. **Legal & Regulatory Limits**: As a legally classified powerboat, your jetski must comply with all applicable waterway rules (e.g., right-of-way, no-wake zones, speed restrictions) enforced by the U.S. Coast Guard and local authorities. 4. **Environmental & Courtesy Restrictions**: Cruising near shorelines, wildlife habitats, swimmers, fishermen, or populated beaches is discouraged; slow down and maintain respectful distances to minimize noise, wake, and environmental impact. For full details, always consult your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual—especially Section 'Cruising Limitations' (page 11) and 'Boating with Passengers' (page 101).

question: "I've heard that my jetski has cruising limitations. Please introduce them."
ret:
Your jetski has specific cruising limitations designed for safety and optimal performance. According to the official documentation, the key cruising limitation is: 

• **Maximum Occupancy & Load**: This watercraft is designed to carry **only the operator and one passenger** — never more than 2 persons. The **maximum total load (operator + passenger + cargo) is 160 kg (353 lb)**. Exceeding this limit compromises stability, handling, and safety. Additionally, you should not operate with a passenger until you have gained considerable solo riding experience, as passenger operation requires greater skill and familiarity with the watercraft’s handling characteristics.

Other critical operational limitations that affect cruising include:

• **Operator Age & Supervision**: jetski recommends a minimum operator age of **16 years**, with adult supervision required for minors. State-specific boating safety course requirements may also apply.

• **Legal Classification & Navigation Rules**: Your jetski is legally considered a **powerboat**, so it must comply with all applicable 'Rules of the Road' — including right-of-way rules (stand-on vs. give-way vessels), no-wake zones, speed restrictions near shorelines, swimmers, wildlife areas, and populated beaches.

• **Responsible Riding Practices**: Cruising must respect others and the environment — avoid excessive noise or wake near homes, nesting areas, fishermen, swimmers, or beaches; maintain safe distances; and never ride in prohibited or ecologically sensitive zones.

For full details, please refer to Section 'Cruising limitations' (page 11) and 'Boating with passengers' (page 101) in your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual.

question: "What are the operation requirements if I want to use my jetski?"
ret: To safely operate your jetski, you must meet the following key requirements: 1. **Operator Eligibility**: Minimum age is 16 years; minors require adult supervision. Check your state’s specific age and boating safety course requirements — many states mandate a certified boating safety course. 2. **Pre-Operation Preparation**: Read the Owner’s/Operator’s Manual, Riding Practice Guide, Riding Instruction card, and all warning/caution labels; watch the Basic Orientation Video. Never allow anyone to operate the jetski without completing these steps. 3. **Safety Gear**: All riders must wear a U.S. Coast Guard–approved personal flotation device (PFD) rated for personal watercraft use. Additionally, wear protective clothing — such as a wetsuit bottom or thick, tightly woven, snug-fitting apparel (e.g., denim) — to prevent injury from forceful water entry; avoid spandex or similar stretch fabrics. 4. **Operational Limits**: Do NOT operate at night — the jetski lacks required lighting for dusk-to-dawn use. Operating after sunset or before dawn significantly increases collision risk. 5. **Legal Compliance**: Jetskis are classified as powerboats — you must follow all applicable federal, state, and local waterway rules (e.g., International Rules of the Road), including right-of-way, steering, and navigation protocols. For full details, consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary or DMV.

question: "What kinds of watercraft characteristics does my jetski have?"
ret:
您的Jet Ski具有以下水craft特性：
1. 推进方式：采用水喷射式（water-jet propelled）推进系统，喷泵（jet pump）与发动机直连，无传统“空挡”（neutral）位置，发动机运转时即产生推力；
2. 安全结构：配备冷却水出水口（用于确认冷却水流）、船首系缆环（bow eye）、前部储物舱、可调节侧鳍（Adjustable Sponson, YAS）、喷射进水口（jet intake）、喷射推力喷嘴（jet thrust nozzle）等关键部件；
3. 法律属性：依法被认定为动力艇（powerboat），须遵守《国际海上避碰规则》及美国海岸警卫队（USCG）和地方水道管理机构的航行法规；
4. 操作限制：建议最低操作年龄为16周岁，未成年人需由成人监督，强烈推荐完成经NASBLA认证的水上安全培训课程（如Jet Ski官方GET W.E.T.项目或Boat/US在线课程）；
5. 安全警示：运行中严禁靠近进水格栅（intake grate）或向喷嘴内插入任何物体，以防卷入旋转部件导致严重伤害或溺水；
6. 推荐随行装备：哨子等声响信号装置、USCG认证的视觉求救信号（如烟火信号或镜面反光器）、计时手表、应急拖曳绳等。

question: "When encountering vessels driving my jetski, what rules should I obey?"
ret:
When encountering other vessels while operating your jetski, you must follow the 'Rules of the Road'—as your jetski is legally considered a powerboat. Three main encounter scenarios require specific actions:

1. **Meeting Head-On**: Both vessels must alter course to starboard (right) to pass port-to-port.
2. **Crossing Situation**: The vessel approaching from your starboard (right) side has the right-of-way—you are the 'give-way' vessel and must slow down or change direction to pass safely behind them.
3. **Overtaking**: You (the overtaking vessel) are always the 'give-way' vessel—signal intent if required, and pass at a safe distance and speed.

General principles:
• The 'stand-on' vessel (with right-of-way) must maintain course and speed—unless immediate collision risk demands evasive action.
• The 'give-way' vessel must take *positive, timely, and visible* action—e.g., slowing, turning, or stopping—to avoid crossing ahead.
• Under the General Prudential Rule: If collision appears unavoidable, *both* vessels must act to avoid it—even if one had right-of-way.
• Always scan constantly, operate at safe speeds, keep safe distances, and never assume other operators see you.

For full, location-specific rules, consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary, Department of Motor Vehicles, or complete a NASBLA-approved boating safety course (e.g., jetski’s free Online Boating Safety Course at jetski-motor.com).

question: "I'd like to remove and install the seat of my jetski, what steps should I follow?"
ret:
To remove the seat: Pull the seat latch up, and then pull the seat off. 

To install the seat: Insert the projection on the front of the seat into the stay on the deck, and then push the rear of the seat down to lock it in place securely.

question: "In daily usage, how to open and close the hood of the jetski?"

To open the hood of the jetski: Push the hood latch <PIC> down, then lift the hood up.

To close the hood: Push the hood down firmly until it locks in place. , ["hood_latch_01"]

question: "I've noticed that there are two different kinds of filler caps on the jetski. How to remove them?"
ret: There are two main filler caps on the jetski: the fuel tank filler cap and the oil tank filler cap. To remove either cap, turn it counterclockwise. Specifically: 1. For the fuel tank filler cap — turn it counterclockwise to remove <PIC>. 2. For the oil tank filler cap (which also serves as a dipstick) — turn it counterclockwise to remove <PIC>. Always ensure both caps are securely tightened before operating the watercraft. , ["EJU01012", "EJU10131"]

question: "If I need to manipulate the two kinds of engine switches on the jetski, what should I do?"
ret:
您好，关于喷射艇（jetski）上两种发动机开关的操作，请注意以下关键安全与操作规范：

1. **主开关钥匙（Main Switch Key）**：
   - 启动前确保 throttle 杠杆完全松开、水艇处于完全静止状态；
   - 启动时将钥匙转至 'START' 位置（勿在发动机运行中重复转动，否则可能损坏启动机构）；
   - 停止发动机时：(1) 先将遥控操纵杆回至中立（Neutral）位置；(2) 再将主开关钥匙转至 'OFF' 位置 <PIC>。

2. **发动机急停开关（Engine Shut-off Switch）及熄火绳（Lanyard）**：
   - 此为紧急安全装置，必须始终连接至操作者身体（如手腕或救生衣）；
   - 一旦意外落水或需紧急停机，拉断熄火绳将立即切断发动机供电，强制停机 <PIC>；
   - 维修/清理进水口或叶轮前，务必先关闭发动机并拔下熄火绳，防止误启动造成严重伤害 <PIC>。

⚠️ 重要警告：
- 切勿在高速运行后立即关停发动机，应先怠速或低速运转数分钟以冷却；
- 发动机停止后即丧失转向控制能力，存在碰撞风险；
- 两种开关均不可带载操作（如运行中切换主开关），且严禁连续启动超过5秒，以免损伤启动电机或耗尽电池。

如您所指‘两种开关’涉及其他特定型号（如巡航辅助开关、倒车RPM控制开关等），请提供具体机型或开关图示，我们将为您进一步定位说明。 , ["drill0_04", "drill0_05", "drill0_06"]

question: "There are two kinds of levers on the jetski, how to manipulate them?"
ret:
Jet ski 上的两种主要操纵杆是：
1. **遥控操纵杆（Remote control levers）**：用于控制前进/倒车及发动机动力输出。向前推动操纵杆，升高管片（shift gates），使全部喷射推力向后，驱动艇体前进；向后拉动则改变推力方向实现倒车或减速。操作前请确保管片无卡滞，全程动作应顺畅（参考文档3、4）。
2. **倾角调节杆（Tilt lever）**：位于方向盘下方，用于调节方向盘倾斜角度，共5档可调。操作步骤为：(1) 向下按压杠杆；(2) 上下移动方向盘至所需位置；(3) 松开杠杆使其自动锁止，并确认方向盘已牢固锁定（参考文档1、6）。⚠️ 重要警告：行驶中严禁触碰倾角调节杆，否则方向盘可能突然移位，导致事故（参考文档1）。
此外，部分型号还配有喷射冲洗手柄（jet wash handle lever），用于停机后排放冷却水（参考文档2）。

question: "What information should I obtain about Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector when using my jetski"
ret:
When using the Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector on your jetski, you should obtain and follow this key information:

1. **Location**: The QSTS selector $\textcircled{1}$ is located at the left handlebar grip.

2. **Function**: It adjusts the trim angle by changing the vertical angle of the jet thrust nozzle — affecting bow-up or bow-down attitude for optimal handling and performance.

3. **Positions**: There are 5 trim positions:
   - 2 bow-down positions ($\circledast$, $\circleddash$),
   - Neutral ($\Lsh$),
   - 2 bow-up positions ($\copyright$, $\circledcirc$).

4. **Safe Operation Procedure**:
   - Reduce engine speed to **3,000 r/min or less** before adjusting.
   - Squeeze the shift lock lever $\circledcirc$, then turn the QSTS selector to your desired position.
   - Release the shift lock lever to lock it in place.

5. **Critical CAUTION**: 
   - ❗ **Never adjust the QSTS selector while operating at full throttle** — doing so may cause damage to the system.

6. **Usage Guidance**:
   - The neutral (“N”) position provides good all-around performance for most conditions.
   - Use bow-down for improved acceleration and planing; bow-up for higher top speed and reduced spray at high speeds.

Always refer to your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual and observe all warning labels for safe, reliable operation.

question: "What do fuel meter and hour meter on my jetski do?"
ret: The fuel meter shows the amount of fuel remaining in the tank—displayed as eight segments that change based on operating conditions; use it as a general reference. When fuel drops to about 13 L, the lowest two segments blink along with the fuel warning indicator and buzzer. The hour meter tracks total engine operating hours since the watercraft was new, helping you follow the maintenance schedule. Note: the hour meter retains elapsed time even if the battery is disconnected, and a malfunction will show "--" and cause the warning light to blink.

question: "Inspecting fuel filter and fuel tank of my jetski is important. How to do that?"
ret:
您好，关于喷射艇（Jetski）燃油滤清器和燃油箱的检查，根据官方手册说明：

1. **燃油滤清器检查与更换**：
   - ⚠️ **严禁自行拆卸或更换燃油滤清器**。错误安装可能导致汽油泄漏，引发火灾或爆炸。
   - 燃油滤清器位于发动机左前方（参考图66），更换周期为每500小时或每年一次（以先到者为准，恶劣环境需更频繁）。
